greet.text: Gentry has become the Factory's nervous system: cables run from his rig down through the floor like roots, and every screen carries a sightline or a circuit. "They're professionals," he reports, not looking up. "Which is good. Professionals have BUDGETS. We are going to be uneconomical, Slick Henry. We are going to be the worst quarter of somebody's fiscal year."

options.text: "Why aren't you panicking? You panic about VISITORS."

calm.text: "Visitors interrupt the work. These people ARE the work." He flips a breaker; somewhere below, a dead line machine shudders half-alive. "Also—" and here the old mania surfaces, wearing armor now, "—they've come to steal the Shape, Slick. The SHAPE. There are things a man discovers he'll do, standing between vandals and the only proof God left lying around." Flip. Flip. "Hand me the yard schematic."

assets.text: "Fence current on the north wire, floods on the yard, the freight door slaved to my board — and your ART, which they will catalog as 'sculpture' in their approach brief." A thin, terrible smile. "Their brief is wrong. Go wire your children into my board, Slick: the Witch on the west approach, the Corpsegrinder in the door, and the Judge—" he hands you the remote like a field commission, "—the Judge goes wherever the Judge feels called. It's HIS day, after all."

options.text: "His day. Yeah. Let's introduce them to the art." (leave)

bye.text: Gentry returns to his board of breakers and sightlines, conducting the Factory like a doomed and glorious orchestra — a man who hunted the Shape of everything for nine years and, on the day of the vandals, finds he has accidentally built a fortress around it.
